agent_reply: Added the one-state-machine state list to [readme.md](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/readme.md). I did not edit `Hotel.h` or `Hotel.cpp`.
Added the one-state-machine state list to [readme.md](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/readme.md). I did not edit `Hotel.h` or `Hotel.cpp`.
Implemented `Hotel` as a namespace-backed power manager in [Hotel.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.h) and [Hotel.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp).
What’s included:
- State machine from the README: blocked, active thresholds, light sleep states, shutdown.
- `pollCore0()` / `pollCore1()` synchronization before light sleep.
- User activity API, including ISR-safe activity notification.
- Blocking while recording, Bluetooth is active, Wi-Fi is active, or audio mode is active.
- Transition-only CPU/backlight changes with debug transition logging.
- `esp_timer_get_time()` wrapper for sleep-safe elapsed time.
- M5 PMIC shutdown, M5 light sleep when ready, timer-only ESP light sleep fallback before `M5.begin()`.
I also wired it into:
- [main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp): both core loops call Hotel polling.
- [Buttons.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Buttons/Buttons.cpp): button activity resets Hotel timer.
- [FlyGui.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp): touch activity resets Hotel timer.

Verification: `pio run` still fails before compiling `Hotel` because of the existing `SdCard` namespace collision with SdFat in `src/mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp`. The error is the same prior blocker: repo `namespace SdCard` conflicts with SdFat’s global `SdCard` typedef.
Fixed in [Hotel.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp:38).

The bug was that `kFullShutdownAllowedByLogging` only blocked `State::Shutdown`; light sleep was still allowed. I added `kLightSleepAllowedByLogging` and made `state_allows_light_sleep()` return `false` when logging is above error.
So debug/info builds can still dim/lower CPU, but they won’t call `M5.Power.lightSleep()` anymore, which is the path that preceded the `RTCWDT_RTC_RESET`.
I did not run a full build; `git diff --check` is clean.
Fixed in [Hotel.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp:38).

The bug was that `kFullShutdownAllowedByLogging` only blocked `State::Shutdown`; light sleep was still allowed. I added `kLightSleepAllowedByLogging` and made `state_allows_light_sleep()` return `false` when logging is above error.
So debug/info builds can still dim/lower CPU, but they won’t call `M5.Power.lightSleep()` anymore, which is the path that preceded the `RTCWDT_RTC_RESET`.
I did not run a full build; `git diff --check` is clean.

user_prompt: I fixed it, all I did was disable CPU frequency changing
so right now the only meaningful thing Hotel can do is dim the screen and shutdown, I think, look at my modification to it
agent_reply: I think your diagnosis is right, but I don’t see the actual CPU-disable change saved in the workspace yet.
Right now [Hotel.cpp](</D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp>) still calls:
```cpp
setCpuFrequencyMhz(state_uses_max_cpu(next) ? kCpuMaxMhz : kCpuMinMhz);
setCpuFrequencyMhz(kCpuMaxMhz);
So the `Hotel.cpp` change I can see is basically line-ending churn, not a semantic change. If your device test worked, the disabling may be in an unsaved editor buffer or a different file/build.
Conceptually, yes: with CPU frequency changes removed and `kLightSleepSupported = false`, Hotel’s meaningful jobs become:
- keep brightness full while active/blocked
- dim brightness after idle time
- optionally power off after `kShutdownMs`, only when logging allows it
- track blocked state from Bluetooth/Wi-Fi/audio so it does not dim/shutdown during active work

The state names `ActiveDimAllowed`, `LightSleepReady`, and `DimLightSleepReady` become a bit stale because there is no CPU scaling and no light sleep. They mostly just mean “idle age bucket” and “brightness bucket” now.

This is a very believable root cause. Changing CPU frequency while Bluedroid is in its HFP/RFCOMM setup window could absolutely produce the “ACL connected, no SLC, timeout later” shape we were seeing.
